pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 1

readAloudText.Drift: The helpers gather on the hill.
readAloudText.Wonder: Nova, Ollie, and Mica fold their soft blanket-capes as the hilltop sky begins to shimmer.
readAloudText.Explore: Nova Glow, Ollie Bluebeam, and Mica Mosslight met on the hill where the sky looked stitched with silver. Their blanket-capes rested softly on their shoulders.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 2

readAloudText.Wonder: A small star-lantern slips from the sky quilt and lands gently near the path.
readAloudText.Explore: From high above, one star-lantern loosened from the sky quilt. It did not crash. It floated down and landed with a tiny hush.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 3

readAloudText.Drift: Ollie asks Nova to notice first.
readAloudText.Wonder: Nova wants to rush, but Ollie asks everyone to notice what the lantern needs first.
readAloudText.Explore: Nova stepped forward fast, cape glowing pink-gold. Ollie lifted one careful hand. “First we notice,” he said. “Then we help.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 4

readAloudText.Wonder: Mica makes a listening map on the grass with soft green light.
readAloudText.Explore: Mica knelt and traced a gentle green circle in the grass. The listening map glowed quietly around the lantern.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 5

readAloudText.Wonder: The star-lantern blinks unevenly, showing it is not hurt but very tired.
readAloudText.Explore: The lantern blinked bright, then dim, then bright again. Nova leaned closer. “It is not broken,” Mica said. “It is tired.”

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 6

readAloudText.Wonder: Ollie spots the tiny silver thread that once tied the lantern to the sky quilt.
readAloudText.Explore: Ollie’s careful eyes found a thread thin as moon hair. It curled from the lantern toward the waiting sky.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 7

readAloudText.Wonder: Nova warms the lantern with a gentle hand instead of pushing it upward.
readAloudText.Explore: Nova wanted to lift the lantern at once. Instead, she placed her palm nearby and let warm gold light settle around it.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 8

readAloudText.Wonder: Mica asks the night wind to slow down so the silver thread can stop trembling.
readAloudText.Explore: Mica faced the small wind. “Softly, please,” they whispered. The grass stopped shivering, and the thread rested.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 9

readAloudText.Drift: They braid a bridge of glow.
readAloudText.Wonder: Together the helpers braid a tiny bridge of glow from hilltop to sky quilt.
readAloudText.Explore: Bluebeam, mosslight, and glow twisted together above the path. The bridge they made was not strong because it was loud. It was strong because it was steady.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 10

readAloudText.Wonder: The lantern rises one handspan, then pauses while everyone waits.
readAloudText.Explore: The star-lantern floated up a little. Nova held her breath. Ollie smiled. “Waiting is helping too,” he said.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 11

readAloudText.Wonder: A cloud drifts in front of the sky quilt, and the helpers keep their lights calm.
readAloudText.Explore: A soft cloud crossed the sky quilt. The path grew dim, but no one panicked. Three small helper lights stayed even and kind.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 12

readAloudText.Wonder: The cloud moves away, revealing the empty stitch where the lantern belongs.
readAloudText.Explore: When the cloud moved on, the missing stitch shone clear. The lantern pulsed as if it recognized home.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 13

readAloudText.Wonder: Nova guides, Ollie steadies, and Mica encourages as the lantern returns to its place.
readAloudText.Explore: Nova’s glow lifted, Ollie’s bluebeam steadied, and Mica’s mosslight hummed encouragement. The lantern rose the last little way.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 14

readAloudText.Wonder: The sky quilt glows whole again, softer than before.
readAloudText.Explore: The silver thread tied itself into a tiny bow. The sky quilt glowed whole again, not brighter in a noisy way, but softer and safer.

pages.illustrationAlt: Text-free illustration for Starblanket Helpers, page 15

readAloudText.Wonder: The helpers fold their capes beside the hill, knowing the bravest power was helping the night feel safe.
readAloudText.Explore: Nova, Ollie, and Mica folded their blanket-capes beside the hill. The strongest bedtime power was not rushing. It was helping the night feel safe.
